nairobi.name: Nairobi Capital
description: Nairobi is home to the African headquarters of over 40 multinational corporations and the ecosystem that produced M-Pesa.

The city represents the primary entry point for institutional capital in East Africa. The structural shortage of premium, secure, and fully serviced accommodation has created a landlord-favorable environment where quality-managed stock routinely achieves 80%+ annual occupancy.

enjoy: Vibrant expatriate communities in Westlands and Kilimani.
Direct proximity to Nairobi National Park and wildlife conservation.
Premium international schools and healthcare infrastructure.
invest: Highly liquid secondary market for stabilized assets.
Strong capital appreciation driven by land scarcity.
USD-denominated corporate leases insulate against FX risk.
Year-round demand unaffected by tourism seasonality.

tatu.name: Tatu City
description: Tatu City is a 5,000-acre Special Economic Zone 30km from Nairobi CBD — Africa's most advanced planned urban development, with its own governance, power, and road infrastructure.
Residential and hospitality land within the SEZ is a fundamentally different risk profile: infrastructure-stage appreciation, not current yield. The thesis is entry price and zoning certainty.

enjoy: Crawford International School and Nova Pioneer campuses.
Uninterrupted power and water supply managed internally.
Secure, gated residential precincts with extensive green spaces.
invest: Zero VAT and import duty exemptions within the SEZ.
Massive capital appreciation as phase 3 infrastructure completes.
High demand from executives working within the SEZ industrial parks.
Streamlined permitting and business registration processes.

mombasa.name: Mombasa Coastal
mombasa.tagline: Coastal leisure with diaspora pull — premium nightly rates.
description: Mombasa's Nyali and Bamburi shoreline is Kenya's premier coastal leisure destination. Tourism is rebounding strongly post-2022 with growing intra-African and European travel.
Boutique serviced villas and apartments command premium nightly rates that outperform Nairobi on a per-night basis — with a strong right-of-use story for investors seeking physical discovery.

enjoy: White sand beaches and Indian Ocean leisure lifestyle.
Rich Swahili cultural heritage and UNESCO-listed Old Town.
Proximity to marine national parks and water sports.
invest: High-yielding short-term rental models targeting affluent tourists.
Strong secondary market demand from the Kenyan diaspora.
Strategic proximity to the Standard Gauge Railway (SGR) terminus.
Hard-currency cash flows during peak international seasons.
